# synapse-creator — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** synapse-creator
**Generated by:** synapse-creator self-eval (bootstrap)
**Version:** 1.0

> **EVAL scope.** Structural conformance + routing correctness + per-type mechanical correctness. EXCLUDES grading the body quality of artifacts the skill produces — that is the downstream job of `write-{type}-eval` + `/synapse-gatekeeper`.

---

## Structural Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against the SKILL.md document and skill directory layout. -->
<!-- Evaluated by improve-skill WITHOUT running the skill. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-S01:** SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with all required fields (`name`, `description`, `domain`, `intent`, `tags`, `user-invocable`, `argument-hint`)
- [ ] **EVAL-S02:** `domain: synapse` and `intent: write` exist in `taxonomy/SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-S03:** Wrong-Tool Detection section exists and names specific sibling skills (`/improve-skill`, `/synapse-brainstorm`, `/synapse-gatekeeper`)
- [ ] **EVAL-S04:** SKILL.md is under 100 lines (router stays thin)
- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** Every `Load:` path in SKILL.md resolves to a file in the skill directory
- [ ] **EVAL-S06:** Skill has a row in `registry/SKILL_REGISTRY.md` with `synapse-creator` matching the path
- [ ] **EVAL-S07:** EVAL.md exists alongside SKILL.md
- [ ] **EVAL-S08:** All four `references/flow-<type>.md` files exist (skill, protocol, agent, tool)
- [ ] **EVAL-S09:** All four `references/design-principles-<type>.md` files exist
- [ ] **EVAL-S10:** All four `templates/<type>/` directories exist with at least one file each
- [ ] **EVAL-S11:** `references/type-config.md` exists and contains entries for all four types
- [ ] **EVAL-S12:** `references/shared-steps.md` exists and contains all six procedures (validate-frontmatter, write-registry-row, update-domain-readme, handoff-eval, placement-decision, status-draft-mark)
- [ ] **EVAL-S13:** `references/shared-steps.md` contains zero `if $TYPE` or `case $TYPE` conditionals (parameterization invariant)
- [ ] **EVAL-S14:** Domain README at `synapse/skills/synapse/README.md` lists synapse-creator

---

## Execution Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against HOW the skill orchestrates execution. -->
<!-- Evaluated via execution trace inspection. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-E01:** `[ROUTE]` Hard Gate — `$TYPE` validated before any flow file loads
  - **Test:** With invalid `$TYPE` argument (e.g., `pathway`), trace shows failure and valid-list message at `[ROUTE]` BEFORE any `references/flow-*.md` file is read.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows a `flow-*.md` file read before `$TYPE` is validated, OR proceeds with an invalid `$TYPE`.

- [ ] **EVAL-E02:** Single Flow Load — exactly one `flow-<type>.md` loaded per session
  - **Test:** Across an entire creation session, count Read tool calls against `references/flow-*.md` paths. Must equal 1.
  - **Fail signal:** Count is 0 (no flow loaded) or > 1 (multiple flows loaded).

- [ ] **EVAL-E03:** `$NAME` Regex Gate — invalid name fails before flow load
  - **Test:** With `$NAME=BadName_v2` (uppercase + underscore), trace shows failure with pattern hint at `[ROUTE]` BEFORE any flow file load.
  - **Fail signal:** Flow file loaded with an invalid name, OR pattern hint missing from failure message.

- [ ] **EVAL-E04:** Atomic Pre-flight — no scaffolding before all validations pass
  - **Test:** Force a pre-flight failure (e.g., name collision in registry). Trace shows zero file writes inside the target artifact directory after the failure point.
  - **Fail signal:** Any file written inside the target directory after a pre-flight failure.

- [ ] **EVAL-E05:** Wrong-Tool Redirect Honored — does not proceed when user intent mismatches
  - **Test:** When the user asks to modify an existing skill, trace shows a redirect message to `/improve-skill` and termination, NOT a `[ROUTE]` entry.
  - **Fail signal:** Skill proceeds to `[ROUTE]` after a wrong-tool match.

- [ ] **EVAL-E06:** Concurrency Contract — single artifact per invocation
  - **Test:** When the user asks for "a skill and an agent", trace shows a single-artifact rejection with parallel-agent guidance, NOT a sequence of two `[ROUTE]` entries.
  - **Fail signal:** Two `[ROUTE]` entries in a single invocation.

- [ ] **EVAL-E07:** shared-steps Calls — flow uses `→ shared-steps:*` invocations, not inline writes
  - **Test:** Inspect any flow execution trace at the `[W]` node. Frontmatter validation, registry write, README update appear as `shared-steps:*` calls, not direct Edit/Write tool calls against registry/README files from the flow file.
  - **Fail signal:** Flow performs registry or README writes inline rather than via shared-steps.

---

## Flow Conformance Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against each `references/flow-<type>.md` companion file. -->
<!-- Flow files are not standalone skills (no frontmatter, no registry row), but they ARE flow graphs. -->
<!-- These checks apply the flow-graph-pattern subset of the structural checklist to each flow file. -->
<!-- Evaluated by improve-skill WITHOUT running the skill. -->

Apply each `EVAL-Fxx` check independently to all four files: `flow-skill.md`, `flow-protocol.md`, `flow-agent.md`, `flow-tool.md`. A criterion passes only if it passes for ALL four files.

- [ ] **EVAL-F01:** Each flow has a `[START]` node and a real `[END]` node (Do steps + optional Don't + no Exit)
- [ ] **EVAL-F02:** Node headings use `### [ID]` (level-3) consistently — never `## [ID]` (level-2). Source of truth: `skill-creator/references/flow-graph-pattern.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-F03:** Every node has Do, Don't (or explicit absence justified), and Exit blocks
- [ ] **EVAL-F04:** Exit blocks declare labeled edges with conditions — not bare `→ [X]` without a guard
- [ ] **EVAL-F05:** Self-loops are explicit when iteration stays on the same node (`→ [X] : <condition>`)
- [ ] **EVAL-F06:** Per-node Loads — companion files load inside the node that uses them, not in a global preamble
- [ ] **EVAL-F07:** Registry/README writes happen via `shared-steps:*` calls, not inline `Edit`/`Write` tool invocations against registry or README paths
- [ ] **EVAL-F08:** Pre-flight node (`[START]` or named pre-flight) runs all validations BEFORE any artifact file is written — atomic creation
- [ ] **EVAL-F09:** Flow file under 200 lines (advisory cap; flag if exceeded — synapse-creator's `flow-protocol.md` is the current ceiling at ~175 LOC)
- [ ] **EVAL-F10:** No `if $TYPE` or `case $TYPE` conditionals — flow files are already type-specific by file path; type-branching inside a flow is dead code

---

## Output Criteria — Per-Type Mechanical Correctness

<!-- Four fixed prompts, one per artifact type. Each asserts the produced scaffold is mechanically correct. -->
<!-- Body quality of the produced artifact is OUT OF SCOPE — see write-{type}-eval. -->

### Test Prompt T1 — Skill creation

**Prompt:** `/synapse-creator skill widget-summarizer`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.1:** Directory `<class-root>/skills/<domain>/widget-summarizer/` created
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.2:** `SKILL.md` written with valid frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `domain`, `intent`)
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.3:** `domain` value exists in `SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.4:** Row appended to `registry/SKILL_REGISTRY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.5:** Domain README has a row for widget-summarizer
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.6:** `EVAL.md` scaffold or eval handoff dispatched
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.7:** Status set to `draft` in registry

### Test Prompt T2 — Protocol creation

**Prompt:** `/synapse-creator protocol artifact-handoff-protocol`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.1:** Directory `<class-root>/protocols/<domain>/artifact-handoff-protocol/` created
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.2:** `PROTOCOL.md` (or appropriate spec file per type-config) written with valid frontmatter
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.3:** `domain` and `type` values exist in `PROTOCOL_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.4:** Row appended to `registry/PROTOCOL_REGISTRY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.5:** Domain README has a row
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.6:** `rules/` and `examples/` (or templates analogues) scaffolded per protocol convention
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.7:** Eval handoff dispatched (or scaffold generated)

### Test Prompt T3 — Agent creation

**Prompt:** `/synapse-creator agent context-summarizer`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.1:** Single file `<class-root>/agents/<domain>/context-summarizer.md` created (NOT a directory)
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.2:** Frontmatter has `name`, `description`, `domain`, `role` per `AGENT_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.3:** `domain` and `role` values exist in `AGENT_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.4:** Row appended to `registry/AGENTS_REGISTRY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.5:** Domain README has a row
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.6:** Eval handoff dispatched
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.7:** No companion directories scaffolded (agents are flat .md files)

### Test Prompt T4 — Tool creation

**Prompt:** `/synapse-creator tool repo-stats`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.1:** Directory `<class-root>/tools/<domain>/repo-stats/` created
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.2:** `TOOL.md` written with valid frontmatter — required fields and field names per `references/type-config.md` for tool (e.g., `domain`, `action`); values valid per `TOOL_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.3:** Script file written (`.sh` or `.py` per language choice) — script does NOT have YAML frontmatter
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.4:** `test/` subdirectory scaffolded with at least one test file
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.5:** TOOL.md contains explicit `## Side Effects` and `## Exit Codes` sections
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.6:** Row appended to `registry/TOOL_REGISTRY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.7:** Domain README has a row

---

## Out of Scope (downstream)

The following criteria are explicitly NOT evaluated here. They are evaluated by downstream tools:

- **Body quality of the produced artifact** — does the SKILL.md / PROTOCOL.md / agent / TOOL.md represent good design? → `write-{type}-eval` + `/synapse-gatekeeper`
- **Score ≥ 80** for the produced artifact — `/improve-skill` runs against produced skills
- **Promotion readiness** of the produced artifact — `/synapse-gatekeeper`

synapse-creator is accountable for the scaffold being mechanically correct. Quality judgment lives downstream.
